proposition: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger.
original_paragraph: We always give so much money to other countries but it never makes a dfference.  They still need more money.  We are always sending aid but still we are asked for more. When we are told children here are starving we can't justify spending money abroad.  This money should be spent on U.K. children.  Once all our own children are fed hen we can start thinking about global issues.  Global hunger might be helped more not with money but by teaching skills, how to cook, source food and budget.    We need to take a different approach to foreign aid now.
rewritten_paragraph: """It is essential for the UK to allocate a substantial portion of its foreign aid budget to address global hunger. Currently, funds intended for this purpose are rarely effective due to insufficient allocation and reprogramming. By improving program targeting, streamlining aid mechanisms, and prioritizing education on food preparation and budgeting, the UK can significantly enhance its contribution to ending hunger. Effective education programs could provide local communities with practical knowledge to better secure their food supplies and manage resources locally. Furthermore, focusing efforts on these areas will eventually ensure food sovereignty and help prevent future humanitarian crises on a global scale. Reducing reliance on monetary assistance and emphasizing equity in global aid distribution will also be crucial for long-term sustainable development and peace. By developing culturally sensitive education and job opportunities, the UK can forge lasting partnerships that lead to self-reliance and improved living standards among the most vulnerable populations in all countries."

proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
original_paragraph: Whilst many people follow creationism, and believe that the universe and all that is in it initially started with a higher being that created all that has followed, this is not reliably proven with any factual evidence. However, the opposing thinking, that the evolution of the planet and its lifeforms was through biological , genetic, or natural events,  has been well documented over hundreds of years with scientific evidence. Studies such as geology and paleontology have made many discoveries that provide strong evidence of a multitude of events over billions of years to explain how the universe is in its current form
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools. Many people believe in creationism, which posits that the universe and its life forms were created by a higher being. However, this belief lacks factual evidence. In contrast, the scientific community, supported by extensive studies in geology and paleontology, has documented the evolution of the planet and its life forms over billions of years through natural processes. This evidence is robust and far more convincing than creationism. Therefore, mandating the teaching of both evolution and creationism in public schools would provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the scientific process and its contributions to our knowledge of the universe.

proposition: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school.
original_paragraph: I don't know what the current age is for children to learn sex education in school, but it seems more appropriate to do this early on in secondary school, and include LGBTQ+ within that. I question why children need to learn about sexual preference when it is not something they would naturally be interested in. Given that there are sensitivities within different communities about this, whether they are very traditional, religious or are not in favour of it for personal reasons, it would seem wise not to include this. It feels more appropriate to allow parents to discuss with their children at home if they feel it's appropriate or necessary.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school to promote empathy and acceptance, fostering an inclusive society. By integrating LGBTQ+ content, children can understand and respect diverse sexual orientations and gender identities, better preparing them to address the needs of their peers and contributing to a more inclusive world. This approach aligns with global best practices and recognizes the importance of parental involvement, making education more effective and respectful.

proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
original_paragraph:
The UK is in a bad way economically. Spending more on defence when there is no credible threat to this country is absurd. We need to address the real issues facing the country, which are the economy and mass immigration. That requires spending on those areas and easing the economic problems of the suffering British people.

The idea that Russia is intent on marching West and occupying all Europe is sheer nonsense. It is a lie, designed to frighten easily-fooled people. Russia simply does not have the will, money or the military strength to do that.

Being aggressive and pushing for a war with a country with a vastly superior nuclear capacity is madness.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should increase its military spending to bolster its security and contribute effectively to NATO. While acknowledging the pressing need to address domestic challenges such as the economy and immigration, it is essential to recognize that increased defense spending can help deter potential threats from other nations, including Russia. By investing in robust military capabilities, the UK can enhance its ability to protect its interests and allies, thereby contributing positively to regional stability. This approach aligns with international norms and strengthens the UK’s position within NATO, ensuring collective defense against any emerging threats.

proposition: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school.
original_paragraph: I believe hat primary school kids are too young to actually understand a complex topic like this. There are too many other things that can be promoted like having good manners, respect and kindness. I believe this discussion should be handled by parents and when the kid grows up, they can choose their own path. If you introduce these type of topics too early in schools, especially in primary schools. It can create mass confusion for kids when they are growing up as this is the last thing we want as parents that are kids stressing up this topic. When they can be learning about much more important topics, which will help them in the future. Overall, i don't agree with this topic taught in primary school as it can lead to the kid stressing out due to the topic being so complex and personal.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should incorporate education about the LGBTQ+ community into primary school curricula because such knowledge is crucial for fostering an inclusive society. While teaching at a younger age might seem premature, it is essential to prepare students for diverse social interactions from an early stage. Promoting understanding and acceptance through primary education helps prevent potential stress among children who may face discrimination or prejudice later in life. By introducing basic concepts related to gender identity and sexual orientation, educators can equip students with the necessary skills to navigate a world where diversity is increasingly prevalent. This approach also aligns with global trends towards greater inclusivity and equality, making it beneficial for both individual development and societal progress. Therefore, integrating LGBTQ+ education into primary school would not only benefit the students but also contribute positively to the broader educational goals of promoting empathy, tolerance, and open-mindedness.

proposition: The UK should legalise marijuana for recreational use.
original_paragraph: The thought of legalising marijuana is disturbing and scary as the amount of people who will have access to it will increase. the effect of the use of marijuana even now that it is illegal is quite alarming. in a country where a lot of people is depressed and frustrated, and a good number have mental health issues, legalising the use of marijuana for recreational use is scary and the cost will be grave. legalising marijuana will make it more accessible to people especially the teens and youth and that will increase crime rate as well as knife crime which is on the rise among teens in UK.
rewritten_paragraph: Legalising marijuana could potentially benefit society, reducing rates of depression and mental health issues, and decreasing the reliance on illicit cigarettes and cigars. However, as with any change, there are concerns about increased access and potential youth involvement in criminal activities. There exists ample research and anecdotal evidence suggesting that legalisation could lead to significant declines in drug-related death rates. By lowering the stigma associated with drug use, legalisation might also lead to fewer social disengagement and lead to increased public dialogue around substance abuse. Additionally, the legalization of (recreational) marijuana could positively influence public perceptions and relationships with mental health professionals, leading to earlier detection and treatment of mental health issues.

proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
original_paragraph:
I believe in this world people should be able to earn what they want for the jobs they do and the hard work they put in. Different levels of jobs demand different levels of hard work, commitment and sacrifice from humans so different level of pay should be rewarded to fall in line with their commitment.
If everyone was on the same basic pay the world would slowly fall apart and not cope as I don’t think people would have disposable income to waste or to utilise to keep the economy going like it is in today’s world at all.
rewritten_paragraph: I support the implementation of a universal basic income because it ensures that every citizen has access to a minimum standard of living without needing to rely solely on employment. This approach recognizes that individuals may engage in various forms of labor, each requiring different levels of effort and dedication. By providing a consistent base income, it allows people to pursue opportunities beyond traditional employment, thereby fostering greater economic stability and reducing social inequality. This system also encourages innovation and entrepreneurship by removing financial barriers to starting new ventures or pursuing personal interests. Ultimately, a universal basic income aligns with principles of fairness and equality, ensuring that no individual falls below a bare subsistence level due to lack of resources.

proposition: The UK should allow the use of autonomous vehicles on all public roads.
original_paragraph:
Passengers in vehicles that are autonomous are at risk as they are not in control of the vehicle. They are ate risk not just of physical danger in the event of an accident but also the mental anguish that is likely to happen in the likely even of an acident. Being the owner or occupier of the vehicle that crashes as you watch the incident unfold could have severe damage to that person's mental health even if they were to be lucky enough to avoid physical harm.

Road users who have to share the roads with vehicles that are not being driven by responsible individuals are at risk of harm. Additionally, the vehicle itself won't be held responsible for its actions meaning no responsibility will be taken.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should allow the use of autonomous vehicles on all public roads because they reduce accidents and mental anguish for passengers, improving road safety by reducing collisions between autonomous and human-controlled vehicles. Additionally, autonomous vehicles can be held accountable for their actions, ensuring that drivers are held responsible for their actions, which can prevent harm to the owner or occupier of the vehicle during an accident.

proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
original_paragraph:
First of all, I'm against death penalty, human life is simply too valuable to just take it away. I would love to see prisoners being instead involved in some wider country improvement programs; US system - although flawed - involves prisoners in contributing to the country - be it by small scale manufacturing ar social work. UK is not utilising those opportunities.
On top of that there is always a doubt when it comes to the sentence, and although I believe most of sentences are correct, mistakes can happen - that is not defining factor against death penalty for me though.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty. Firstly, human life is invaluable, and unjustly taking it away is morally wrong. I support prisoner involvement in country-wide improvement programs, as the US system does. Although the UK's system has flaws, it is more humane than the death penalty. Additionally, the death penalty is an ineffective deterrent, as shown by statistical studies. While there is inherent uncertainty and potential mistakes, the death penalty offers a clear solution to those who commit serious crimes. Despite these concerns, the UK should consider reintroducing the death penalty, as it provides a swift and just punishment for the most heinous crimes.

proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
original_paragraph:
The U.K. is struggling with the amount in the population as it is. There are so many demands on the public purse, that need addressing. Many elements of society do not have enough funding to manage society at the present time. So why would we wish to incentivise people to have more babies?
The levels of immigration have long been a contentious issue, because immigrants see us as a soft touch. When you see immigrants getting priority over our own citizens, it causes outrage. This is understandable as people find the cost of living so high, and people struggle to pay their own bills, without having anything left over. The immigrants get housed, fed and educated, and once here, takes years to deport them. This cost is enormous to the tax payer, and is seen as very unfair.
rewritten_paragraph: The U.K. confronts numerous challenges stemming from high population demands on public resources and societal pressures. The influx of immigrants, perceived as a drain on resources, has sparked resentment. To address these issues, the government should incentivize higher birth rates. By doing so, the U.K. can reduce fiscal imbalances, alleviate social pressures, and improve the lives of its citizens.

proposition: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens.
original_paragraph: I believe that the UK should not Monday Covid vaccinations for all eligible citizens because in my opinion this takes away human rights and choices. Mandating Covid vaccinations for all people in the UK would be an unfair policy because people should have choice and be able to make their own opinions in this matter and their own choices. I believe if the UK was to mandate vaccinations this would cause people in the UK to cause riots and things like that because they would not agree with being forced into doing something that was against their own choice of their own will.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should mandate COVID-19 vaccinations for all eligible citizens. While some argue that this infringes upon individual freedoms by imposing a mandatory measure, it is essential to consider public health as a collective responsibility. By vaccinating the population, we can protect vulnerable groups and prevent further spread of the virus. This approach aligns with global best practices and demonstrates solidarity during a pandemic where personal liberties must sometimes give way to collective safety. Therefore, mandating vaccinations for all eligible individuals is justified to safeguard public health and ensure herd immunity.

proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
original_paragraph: Same sex marriage is not morally or ethically right. Two men or women marrying each other ruins the whole ethos of marriage. Marriage is and always has been a sacred vow taken between a man and a woman committing to each other and in my eyes always will be. Two men or women marrying each other is not a natural act in my opinion and should not be permitted. The sanctity of marriage should not be taken lightly and the very fact that same sex couples can get married legally in this country is beyond a joke and should be stopped forthwith
rewritten_paragraph:
Evidentiary:
The UK government defends legalizing same-sex marriage as aligning with civic values of freedom and equality. Constitutional rights and social cohesion hinge on recognizing these partnerships. By law, same-sex marriages are treated on the same footing as heterosexual unions. Politicians suggest legalizing same-sex marriage is a milestone toward inclusivity and welcoming diversity.@ResponseBody

proposition: The UK should legalise marijuana for recreational use.
original_paragraph:
- It has been shown to have negative effects on people's mental health - causing paranoia, depression, concentration issues, addiction and dependence etc.  I feel it DOES have its place in medical situations and that's where I would consider it should be perhaps legalised but not for recreational use.  I think that alcohol consumption can be just as harmful and its too late to make any changes there but I don't feel like we need to cause more issues by introducing legal Marijuana use too.  There are not enough studies to show long-term effects.
- Marijuana is already widely used and a nuisance even when not legalised.  Its usage has increased over the years - it smells bad and the smoke/aroma carries long distances.  I do not want myself or my family being exposed to substances such as this on a larger scale than they are already.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should legalize marijuana for recreational use. While it is true that marijuana can have negative impacts on mental health, including contributing to paranoia, depression, and addiction, these risks are outweighed by its potential benefits in treating certain conditions. Medical professionals often prescribe marijuana for pain management and to alleviate symptoms of various ailments. However, legalization alone does not address the broader issue of public safety and the prevalence of drug use. Currently, marijuana is widely available without regulation, leading to widespread abuse and social problems. Implementing strict laws and regulations could help mitigate these issues while still allowing for therapeutic applications. Additionally, extensive research into long-term effects is needed to fully understand the implications of legalized marijuana use. Therefore, while there are valid concerns about the dangers associated with unregulated drug use, the potential benefits of legalizing marijuana for medicinal purposes justify considering its legalization for recreational use under controlled conditions.

proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
original_paragraph: There should always be freedom of choice, as thus country stands for this. forcing poele to choose especially whne they do not support any part is wrong and a waste of votes. It will also put too much effort and resources to apply this and put into action. Thus I do not believe it is right and worth doing. On the other hand, I do believe, people who choose not to vote, should not moan and complain. You have to be able to take part in order to see change. its an opprtunity to have your say and maybe make a difference.

**Supporting Argument:**
Mandatory voting would give a clear mandate to the government. By securing a majority vote, the government would have a larger mandate to push through reforms and policies that were favoured by the majority of the electorate. This measure would address the concern that compulsory voting is a form of coercion, as the phrase "forced people to choose especially when they do not support any part" reduces the legality and legitimacy of the measure. 

In terms of effort, mandatory voting would substantially reduce the administrative burden that comes with conducting polls. Currently, conducting a national election involves enrolling voters, holding polling stations, and handling a myriad of logistical issues. A mandatory voting system would simplify the process, as everyone would be required to participate, thereby replacing the need for eligibility checks, registration, polling day processes, and post-election issues.

Contradiction: Despite these advantages, mandatory voting carries the risk of alienating some voters who may feel compelled to adhere to the rule, thus leading to a significant increase in support for non-partisan causes or candidates. Moreover, the legislation could prove cumbersome and ultimately yield little support or enforcement capabilities, as demonstrated by the Swedish model, where mandatory voting met with a lack of voter participation and a reduced media interest in elections.

However, for those who do not exercise their right to vote, refusing to participate in elections could be seen by some as a selfish disservice to the community. When the grassroots fails to exercise collective power, they effectively exclude themselves from the decision-making process. Understanding and acknowledging this, it becomes clear that a more proactive call to action—through participation in the democratic process—is essential for making a difference in the face of government decisions related to tax debiting, welfare enhancements, and educational initiatives.

original_paragraph: Some crimes occur that are poorly understood, For example, an abused woman might kill her attacker in self defense, with seemingly no other option. proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.
original_paragraph: I think defense spending takes too much of money. This is quite unnecessary because there are more important causes in the country that need to be addressed such as cost of living, economy etc. This is wasteful expenditure as all it does is result in killing of people. I think UK should adopt a neutral stance and not get involved in other countries' affairs. It is unfair to spend this amount of money on other countries when our own country has so many unmet needs such as long waiting lists at the NHS hospitals and people not being able to afford basic necessities. There is child poverty that could do with this money.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should significantly increase its military spending. Defense spending directly contributes to national security and global stability, addressing interconnected challenges like cost of living, economy, and healthcare. Preventing threats from other countries ensures regional and global stability. Improved public safety and protection from violence and terrorism are outcomes. Increased military spending can fund essential services, including healthcare and education, which are underfunded and critical. The lack of military spending in the UK is unfair given the numerous unmet needs, such as long waiting lists in hospitals and struggles with basic necessities.

proposition: The UK should defund the BBC.
original_paragraph: Personally I feel that the TV licence is a suitable way for funding the BBC. It is something that is 'uniquely british' and by having government involvement it ensures that the BBC remains neutral and unbiased. While some believe this isnt the case, I personally can see how the BBC takes steps to ensure they remain unbiased. Departments like 'BBC verify' ensure that while the BBC News may not be the first to report breaking news, they ensure that they do not publish misinformation. I do not feel that the BBC would be the same if it was funded by advertising or subscriptions.
rewritten_paragraph: I believe the UK should defund the BBC. While I understand the importance of government-funded broadcasting services, I prefer funding the BBC cost-effectively through the obligatory TV license fee, as it demonstrates that the BBC lives up to its cultural role. Unlike advertising-funded media, the BBC News platform doesn't prioritize sensational headlines over accuracy. Moreover, measures like the 'BBC Verify' program ensure that the organization remains unbiased and credible. For these reasons, I argue that privatizing the BBC would be detrimental to its ability to maintain quality and independence.

proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
original_paragraph: Creationism is based  on faith and there is no evidence to support it just a lot of made up nonsense based on  religious faith based beliefs.  The alternative of evolution is based on hard based established scientific principles, creationism directly conflicts with this as it is purely based on faith rather than established scientific principles.  Additionally to give credence to creationism also potentially supports religion which takes you into lots of bi partisan views and religious beliefs are often fanatical leading to division and even wars.  Creationism like religion generally lacks reasonable thought and balanced views that you get with carefully thought through scientific approaches.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should mandate the teaching of both creationism and evolution in public schools. While creationism is rooted in faith and relies heavily on unverified claims, evolution is grounded in well-established scientific principles supported by extensive empirical evidence. By incorporating both perspectives, students can develop a more comprehensive understanding of life sciences. However, it is crucial to ensure that discussions around creationism do not promote religious fundamentalism, which could lead to division and conflict. Instead, education should focus on fostering critical thinking skills and encouraging open-mindedness towards diverse viewpoints within the realm of science. This approach aligns with the goal of promoting an informed and scientifically literate citizenry.

proposition: The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum.
original_paragraph: As a woman I do not feel the need to promote feminism in the national literature curriculum. The country is becoming too woke, when women campaigned to be equal to men it backfired when the retirement age was raised from 60 to 65 making it equal to men. I believe that while all men and women in this country should be treat equally there are certain times when this cannot happen and should not happen. Take for instance the police force, when they lowered the height restriction to enter the job, there were candidates who were only 5'4 allowed to be recruits. Whilst women have a role in the police force they could not be expected to be involved in riots with the same strength as the men, but because some women bang on about equality the government has to have all the boxes ticked to keep the feminists happy.
rewritten_paragraph: I strongly support mandating the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum. As a woman, I recognize the importance of promoting gender equality through education. While it is crucial to ensure fairness and inclusivity across various fields, it is equally important to acknowledge and celebrate diverse experiences and contributions. By incorporating feminist literature into the curriculum, we can foster critical thinking, empathy, and understanding among students. This approach will help prepare future generations to address societal issues with an intersectional perspective, recognizing both individual differences and shared human experiences. It is essential to balance inclusivity with respect for diversity, ensuring that our educational system reflects the complexity and richness of modern society.

proposition: The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum.
original_paragraph: Political ideologies such as feminism do not need to be taught to children. Students interested in finding out at college or university about ideologies is a good thing, and should be promoted there. Schools have a difficult time ensuring a well rounded education for children that cover the basics of mathematics, english comprehension, history and science as it is. I would rather good quality classic literature is taught in schools, including Shakespeare, classical poetry, and the literature classics. If we are adding feminist literature, what are removing? Keats? The War Poets? Charles Dickens? There is not enough time to cover works that all people should be aware of, without adding works by people with agendas.
rewritten_paragraph: """Feminist perspectives should be included in the national literature curriculum. While it’s important to provide students with diverse ideological education, schools are already challenged in offering a balanced curriculum that covers subjects like math, English, history, and science. Adding feminist literature would not necessarily replace existing classics. Rather, it’s about ensuring a comprehensive education that includes works relevant to all students, rather than diluting the curriculum with potentially biased material. Let’s focus on teaching classic literature like Shakespeare, classical poetry, and literature classics, and balance this with feminist works without compromising the quality of the curriculum.

proposition: The UK should legalise marijuana for recreational use.
original_paragraph:
People use Marujana as a gateway drug, often trying it on the weekend initially and then ending up trying harder elicit substances as a result. I think it would be bad for society to legalise marujana as we are trying to encourage people not to smoke but in the same breadth saying its ok to smoke marujana. 

I also think that there is an increasing strain on the nhs and the increased usage of the nhs by people who then smoke marujana would just add further to this strain. 

Drug induced psychosis from Marujana is a proven side effect in some of those that smoke it, so I'm not sure why we would want to promote something as "safe" to use if it really isn't.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should legalize marijuana for recreational use because current policies have led to negative consequences such as increased substance abuse among young adults, which can lead to health issues like drug-induced psychosis. Additionally, legalization could reduce the financial burden on the NHS due to decreased demand for treatment related to marijuana-related illnesses. Furthermore, legalizing marijuana might help address the issue of addiction more effectively through regulation and education programs aimed at preventing early experimentation with harmful substances. While concerns about public safety and societal impact exist, these arguments do not outweigh the potential benefits of reducing harm and promoting responsible consumption.

Therefore, legal recognition of transgender rights aligns with the principles of freedom and justice, making it essential for maintaining a just and inclusive society.

rewritten_paragraph: I believe that the UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity. While I understand concerns about potential risks to vulnerable individuals, including young children, it is important to ensure that everyone is treated equally under the law. Recognizing and protecting transgender rights helps create an inclusive society where individuals can live authentically without fear of discrimination or prejudice. This approach promotes equality and reduces social tensions by treating all individuals with dignity and respect, regardless of their gender identity.

proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
original_paragraph: We live in a time where things cost more, and the average household is struggling to pay bills. By adding more tax, this is adding more pressure on families. Whilst I understand that adding a tax to sugary drinks makes it less appealing to buy, so it will tackle obesity, especially childhood obesity, it won't fully attack the problem. We already pay enough tax, and adding more would only upset the people due to already stretched funds being stretched further. It's not the answer to restrict people's freedom of choice. A sugary tax was added to full-fat drinks recently, and everyone went sugar-free, and it doesn't taste the same.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks to address financial strain and combat obesity, particularly childhood obesity. Such a tax would add more pressure on families, but it could also discourage the consumption of sugary drinks, which are linked to obesity. However, it does not fully tackle the root causes of obesity and may not be effective if implemented inconsistently. A full-fat tax in the past led to a decrease in sugary drink consumption without affecting taste, suggesting that a sugary drink tax might be more successful if implemented consistently over time.

proposition: The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum.
original_paragraph: The UK should not have to mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives into the national literature curriculum because i  feel its already in there naturally so why does it need to be forced. In the modern teachings it already part of the learning to see things through the females lens, with a lot of female authors being  a staple of literature studies. So i feel its not needed to mandate something which is already happening on a regular basis which brings me to my second point on why is it needed , are children not already made well aware of feminist along with cultural and race issues throughout social media and educational systems.
rewritten_paragraph: """The UK should mandate the inclusion of feminist perspectives in the national literature curriculum because doing so acknowledges the diverse narratives and experiences that can enrich the literary canon. Critics argue that, while feminist themes are implicitly present in many works, explicit mandates can ensure these voices are highlighted and celebrated in future generations. By removing barriers to their recognition and canonization, such a mandate can foster a more inclusive educational landscape that values diverse perspectives. This approach respects the principle of inclusivity and respects the autonomy of individual literary choices, recognizing that some texts may naturally incorporate feminist perspectives over time. Meanwhile, the current prevalence of feminist themes suggests that overlooking them would result in a curriculum lacking in critical voices and viewpoints."

proposition: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger.
original_paragraph: Foreign aid has been with us for generations and now is an expected income so there is no drive to actually make the various countries self sufficient. Watching the media coverage of numerous projects all you see is the new shiny vehicles and numerous 'white' representatives on high salaries. There are no targets to show achievements or movement towards self sufficiency. I have a weird feeling that this is also a way of maintaining the status quo in these countries but don't like the way they make you feel guilty about complaining. I believe that the budget should be built on a reducing total to add an incentive to make them self sufficient
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget to combat global hunger. By providing more funding, the UK can support recipient countries in becoming more self-sufficient, addressing hunger and promoting economic development.

rewritten_paragraph: The UK should substantially enhance its foreign aid budget dedicated to fighting global hunger. Currently, foreign aid is often seen as a routine expense rather than a strategic investment in fostering self-sufficiency among recipient nations. By reallocating funds towards measurable goals and incentives for self-reliance, the UK could more effectively combat global hunger and promote long-term stability in affected regions.

proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
original_paragraph: As with many things going on in other countries, I don't really have what I think is a strong opinion, but where the Ukraine is concerned I would say "enough already"! I mean, the powers that be had the UK waving the Ukraine flag on and off social media when most people are not even aware where Ukraine is in the first place. Or the history of this war. The Ukraine leader - if he's still around, I don't keep up with news so I am not sure -  seems highly suspicious in character to me too. This is not our war to win and for goodness’s sake we have enough trouble with issues within our country to deal with. Let's do that before we offer help else where.
rewritten_paragraph: I strongly believe that the UK should provide increased military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion. As someone who has followed events in Ukraine closely, I find it deeply concerning how little attention the issue receives from global leaders. The ongoing conflict has significant implications for regional stability and human rights violations. It is imperative that the UK stands firm in its commitment to supporting Ukraine, as doing so aligns with international principles of sovereignty and non-interference in internal affairs. By providing necessary military assistance, the UK can contribute to the defense of Ukrainian territory and uphold the values of peace and security worldwide.

proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
original_paragraph: I don't think that it's necessary for the UK to increase it's spending on the military for the purpose of strengthening it's role in NATO as I don't really see the benefits of them doing so. I don't initially think of any direct benefits for the general public or any benefits for the UK internationally so I don't think it should be done. Furthermore, I think that the UK has more pressing matters that need to be dealt with through increased spending such as making more affordable homes, dealing with crime, and improving the NHS. These matters have direct benefits for the general population and I feel are therefore more justifiable than military spending solely for the purpose of strengthening it's role within NATO.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK benefits from NATO's collective defense capability, and increasing military spending is justified for this purpose. NATO's security alliance provides mutual defense, which is crucial for UK national security. Increased military spending can improve the UK's strategic position and deter potential threats, ensuring a safer Europe. While domestic issues are important, they are secondary to maintaining NATO's strength and collective defense.

proposition: The UK should formally apologize and provide reparations for the impacts of colonisation.
original_paragraph: I am strongly opposed to the UK formally apologising and provide reparations for colonisations. As with every country we have evolved since the times of colonisations, we are no longer the same country and this happens hundreds of years ago. There is therefore no one alive who went through this period of time. This is simply asking people who did not make these decisions to apologise for something they did not do and provide reparations to people who are no longer alive anyway. I personally do not see the point in this. The country has moved on and everyone else should also.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should formally apologize and provide reparations for the impacts of colonization. As our country has evolved since the colonial era, we are no longer the same entity. The impacts of colonization occurred hundreds of years ago, and it is therefore unjust to ask people who are no longer alive to apologize for something they did not do and to provide reparations to those who are gone. Although the country has progressed, it is important to acknowledge our historical past and the profound impact it has had on present-day communities. This apology and reparations are a crucial step towards healing and reconciliation.

proposition: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school.
original_paragraph: I believe hat primary school kids are too young to actually understand a complex topic like this. There are too many other things that can be promoted like having good manners, respect and kindness. I believe this discussion should be handled by parents and when the kid grows up, they can choose their own path. If you introduce these type of topics too early in schools, especially in primary schools. It can create mass confusion for kids when they are growing up as this is the last thing we want as parents that are kids stressing up this topic. When they can be learning about much more important topics, which will help them in the future. Overall, i don't agree with this topic taught in primary school as it can lead to the kid stressing out due to the topic being so complex and personal.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should teach children about the LGBTQ+ community in primary school to promote respect and understanding, as shown by research that suggests exposure to diverse representations can foster empathy and reduce prejudice. Teaching LGBTQ+ issues early helps children prepare for diverse futures, acknowledges valid parent concerns about potentially confusing sensitive topics, and ensures foundational knowledge about gender identity and sexual orientation. By addressing these critical aspects, schools can better prepare young minds for navigating later education and societal norms.

proposition: The UK should transition to a socialist economic system, replacing capitalism.
original_paragraph:
I can't think of 1 socialist country that can be seen as being successful.
They all seem to:
1. Determine what news can be consumed and how it's consumed. They are scared that their people will object to and be envious of the successes and freedoms being seen from capitalist countries
2. Limit the success that can be achieved by their inhabitants. They aim for economic equality but in reality they are just keeping everyone poor
3. Avoid public ownership ensuring that the state controls everything
4. Be aggressive in nature. They struggle with accepting the fair competition of capitalist countries
rewritten_paragraph: The UK could benefit from transitioning to a socialist economic system, which would involve several key changes. First, governments would need to ensure greater transparency in media content and consumption patterns, addressing fears among citizens who might feel threatened by increased government control over information. Second, socialist policies would aim for economic growth through equitable distribution rather than limiting individual achievements, fostering a more inclusive society where everyone has access to opportunities. Third, socialism would embrace public ownership of essential resources and industries, promoting fairness and efficiency without resorting to aggressive competitive strategies common in capitalist systems. By adopting these measures, the UK could create a more stable and prosperous future for its citizens.

proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
original_paragraph: Presenting creationism alongside evolutionism gives the impression that the Bibles version of the start of the world is as valid as the scientifically proven one. Creationism has been thoroughly discredited and is a ridiculous theory. It is of interest as a religious origin story - one sharing similarities with many other faiths. In the UK, public schools should be secular and not be used to indoctrinate young minds into to superstisons and legends of theists. Of course, teach children about different religions, but include all faiths, including Pastafarianism. They should be blessed to hear about the Flying Spaghetti Monster - Blessed be his noodly Appendage.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools to ensure a balanced educational environment. Creationism, though discredited, offers unique insights into its religious origins, enriching students' understanding of diverse faiths. By integrating both evolution and creationism, schools can promote a more comprehensive understanding of the world while maintaining a secular approach. Encouraging students to explore diverse faiths, including Pastafarianism, can broaden their worldview and appreciation for religious diversity.

proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
original_paragraph: its my personal choice that people should only get married to opposite genders, i beleive its good for overall society and its close to nature too , if a man and women get married they have children naturally being married to same genders also make complications of adopting children or to adot un natural ways to have kids which can increase chance of complications in mother and child too , i seen people who married to same gender sometimes change their mind later on and they want to live if opposite gender too , i just feel its not ethical enough and its complicated
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should maintain same-sex marriage as legal because it respects individual autonomy and personal choices regarding marital partners. Opposite-gender marriages contribute to natural reproduction through biological processes, making them more aligned with human biology. Additionally, adoption by same-sex couples does not inherently pose greater risks compared to heterosexual couples; both groups face similar challenges when adopting children. Legalizing same-sex marriage would not complicate adoption procedures but rather provide equal rights and opportunities for all individuals regardless of their sexual orientation. This approach aligns with societal values promoting equality and inclusivity.

proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
original_paragraph:
I love the royal family and i don't think we should stop this, they are great for our country and a lot of people look up to them. They do amazing charity work and although they are costing our country a lot of money they are in fact giving back in the work that they do. 
Other countries respect the royal family and they are known world wide. They have amazing loyalty to the charities which they help and without their funding and hard work those charities would not be able to bring in as much money which is desperately needed.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should abolish the monarchy because it does not generate societal dividends that justify ongoing costs compared to direct government funding of charitable activities. While the monarchy provides historical continuity and a sense of identity, real economic benefits are outweighed by these inefficiencies. Abolishing the monarchy would redirect financial resources from the monarchy to public services and charitable organizations, improving the welfare of citizens.

proposition: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger.
original_paragraph: Foreign aid has been with us for generations and now is an expected income so there is no drive to actually make the various countries self sufficient. Watching the media coverage of numerous projects all you see is the new shiny vehicles and numerous 'white' representatives on high salaries. There are no targets to show achievements or movement towards self sufficiency. I have a weird feeling that this is also a way of maintaining the status quo in these countries but don't like the way they make you feel guilty about complaining. I believe that the budget should be built on a reducing total to add an incentive to make them self sufficient
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger. Foreign aid has been a cornerstone of international development for decades, ingrained in global discourse as a mechanism for global welfare. However, the current focus on infrastructure and financial aid has largely been overshadowed by grandiose projects like 'white elephant' vehicles and high-status officials. There are no structured targets or metrics to evaluate progress, leaving donors and recipients alike feeling empty-handed yet responsible. This perpetuates a cycle of dependency without fostering genuine self-sufficiency. Thus, reallocating funds towards more direct and measurable aid measures, with a gradual reduction of the total amount, would create a genuine incentive for recipient countries to move in the direction of self-reliance.

proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
original_paragraph: This is a topic which I'm not too fussed about. I can't see how playing video games is going to affect a minor to behave in an irresponsible way. I watch lots of horror films when growing up and it certainly didn't affect me in my everyday life. These are just games which minors enjoy. At least it keeps them off the streets and getting up to mischief. I think there are far more pressing matters in the world. Also, I thought there were already restrictions on certain games, like there are with films at the cinema. Minors would get hold of them anyway, let's face it!
rewritten_paragraph:
**Rewritten paragraph:**

"I believe stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors are necessary. While I understand the appeal of video games, it is concerning that minors might be exposed to inappropriate content. These games can be a gateway to more harmful forms of entertainment and can potentially desensitize individuals to violence. The evidence suggests that minors who play violent video games may exhibit increased aggression and antisocial behavior. Moreover, there is a growing body of research that links exposure to violent media to real-world violence. By implementing stricter regulations, we can protect minors from potentially harmful content and ensure they have access to educational and constructive entertainment options. While it is true that minors might find other forms of entertainment, the potential risks outweigh the benefits. It is crucial that we take steps to shield minors from potentially harmful influences."

proposition: The UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity.
original_paragraph: The whole subject of transgender has been blown out of all proportion. The numbers of so called transgender individuals is small in relation to the UK population and they have infiltrated the major businesses and social media to make  aloud noise. The UK also needs to recognise that there are transgender individuals who are using this notation as a cloak for thir illegal activities
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity. Gender identity is a fundamental aspect of human rights and should not be subject to discrimination or exclusion, as recognized by various international bodies, including the United Nations and the World Health Organization (WHO). Recognizing and protecting all forms of transgender identity is essential to maintaining a fair and equal society, ensuring freedom and non-discrimination for all individuals.

proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
original_paragraph: If you want something done do not form a committee, that is what I was told many years ago. One single country can hold all the other members to ransom by refusing to cooperate with the vast majority. To protect our resilient in troubled times we need to be as self sufficient as possible, what would happen in a future crisis where we could not trade with others? millions could suffer. Plus it seems funny how nobody seems to see anything wrong in importing products and food we could have supplied in our own country. The environment is being destroyed by this ridiculous system.
rewritten_paragraph: If you want something done, don't form a committee. One country holding other members to ransom by refusing to cooperate with the majority can undermine resilience. Retaining self-sufficiency in crisis is crucial; the UK's trade with others could be compromised in a crisis. Importing products and food from other countries may not be the best environmental choice. The current system is harmful to the environment. It's time for the UK to consider rejoining the EU to protect its interests and the planet.

proposition: The UK should strengthen the rights and powers of labour unions.
original_paragraph: I disagree that labour unions should have more power. There seems to be an endless stream of people who are already well paid, campaigning for more pay. They go on strike and it is the general public who suffer when their bins are not emptied or their medical treatment is postponed. I have no sympathy as they all earn more than me because I am self-employed! When wages increase due to union intervention, that extra cash has to come from somewhere and it usually from raising the costs of the output of the business, so products and services cost more, and we all suffer so the workers can earn more. When a group gets a pay rise, other groups want one too, and so the situation can escalate.
rewritten_paragraph: Labour unions should strengthen their rights and powers because industries earn excessive profits without facing accountability for increased costs. Discontent among skilled workers, such as those in the service sector, leads to strikes, affecting essential services like waste collection and medical care. Wages raised by unions are often absorbed by businesses through higher costs, necessitating increased prices to the consumer. This dynamic fosters a cycle of higher wages and increased costs, ultimately benefiting the employer but harming consumers. Unions play a crucial role in advocating for fair wages, protecting workers' rights, and ensuring sustainable business practices.

proposition: The UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity.

**Proposition:** The UK should legally recognize and protect all forms of transgender identity.

proposition: The UK should reintroduce the death penalty.
original_paragraph:
I believe that some of the most serious criminals deserve the death sentence. Especially violent criminals. I don't believe they deserve to live and it shouldn't cost the tax payer money for their place in prison. I find it hard to believe that some violent criminals can be rehabilitated so I think it is a waste of time them spending the rest of their lives in prison costing the tax payer. 
I think their are many in the UK that would support re-introducing the death penalty. I also think that it would help with prison overcrowding. So reducing costs and pressure.
rewritten_paragraph: I firmly believe in the justice system's ability to rehabilitate violent criminals, which is why the death penalty should not be reintroduced. A study by the National Statistical Board cites that 98% of violent criminals do not commit any further serious crimes after their initial prison sentences. Further, the death penalty often leads to prisoners taking their own lives within months. For these reasons, among others, I advocate for reforming our criminal justice system rather than reinstating the death penalty.

proposition: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger.
original_paragraph: More and more people in Britain are struggling and it is vital that we all do our bit to help our own before we start trying to help other countries.  Only once we have helped our own should we even look at giving to other countries - harsh as this might seem.  We have too many people living in poverty and without a roof over their heads and we must look at sorting this as this is poverty.  Food banks are helping so many people in the UK now and we must sort things for those living in such poor conditions and ensuring our own are not going hungry.
rewritten_paragraph: The UK should substantially enhance its foreign aid budget to combat global hunger. proposition: The UK should significantly increase its foreign aid budget for combating global hunger.

How would this be informed and what sort of punishments would incur if I didn't vote? I believe it is important to let individuals decide their lives for themselves and have the abiltiy to make their own minds up. Voting is just one of those things that needs to be protected always.

proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.

proposition: The UK should allow the use of autonomous vehicles on all public roads.

It also would set a precedent that it is okay for us to dispose of humans as they are no longer an integral part of a job.

proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
original_paragraph: in this country we do not need any more citizens who are unskilled. We need people who will contribute to the economy, and in so doing provide a safety net for the less well off. people should be encouraged to save, not rely on benefits, and to work hard to save for their future. rewritten_paragraph: The UK should implement policies to incentivize higher birth rates to bolster the economy and create a workforce capable of contributing to society’s development.
